entity.name: Button
entity.description: An interactive element that triggers an action when activated. Buttons communicate what will happen when the user interacts with them.

items.description: When the user needs to trigger an action such as submitting a form, saving data, or confirming a decision.
When the action navigates the user to a different page or URL.

alternative.rationale: Links carry native navigation semantics. Screen readers announce them as links, and browsers support open-in-new-tab.

items.guidance: Limit each surface to one primary button.
items.rationale: Multiple primary buttons dilute visual hierarchy. When everything is emphasized, nothing is.

items.guidance: Use a verb or verb phrase that describes the action the button performs.
items.rationale: Action-oriented labels set clear expectations about what will happen on activation.

agentDocumentBlocks.purpose: Trigger a user-initiated action within the current view without causing page navigation.

items.description: Use button for in-page actions; use link for navigation to a URL.

items.guidance: Do not use for navigating to a different page or URL.
items.rationale: Buttons signal actions and links signal navigation. Assistive technology announces each differently, and users rely on that distinction to predict what a control will do.

items.guidance: Limit each surface to one primary-emphasis button.
items.rationale: A single primary action keeps the visual hierarchy clear. When several buttons compete at primary emphasis, the intended next step becomes ambiguous.
